response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong testing discipline and broad polyglot experience, but the resume shows no modern C++ or Rust production work — only brief C/C++ component work at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015) and a C++ bachelor thesis. The role requires modern C++ (or Rust) as the primary language, plus geospatial/navigation pipeline experience, neither of which are demonstrated at the depth expected for a Senior SDE on a routing tiling team. GIS exposure via GISCollective is a positive signal but is built in DLang/Node.js, not the target stack.
strengths: Long-running GIS platform (GISCollective) shows genuine geospatial domain interest
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, low-latency systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Polyglot engineer comfortable picking the right tool and learning new stacks
concerns: No modern C++ or Rust experience; C/C++ work is ~11 years old and limited in scope
No navigation/routing pipeline or map-tiling background
Recent roles are TypeScript/Node-centric, far from the systems-level C++ focus of the role
